items.title: Berkshire Hathaway Has Had a Tough Summer. Will It Get a Buffett Swansong Boost?
items.description: Warren Buffett isn’t used to lagging behind the market.  The billionaire’s  Berkshire Hathaway  investment group has firmly outpaced the  over the past five years, and has delivered staggering returns since he began converting it from a textile company into an acquisition machine in the mid-1960s.  Buffett, who turns 95 later this month, announced his effective retirement last spring by naming Greg Abel, a longtime Berkshire colleague, as his successor.

items.title: Final Agenda: LA CorpGov Forum Sept 4 Featuring Sports and Entertainment

items.title: What happens if JPM buys Apple Card from Goldman
items.description: Investing.com -- JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) could regain the top spot in U.S. credit cards if it acquires Goldman Sachs’ Apple Card portfolio, a potential $17 billion deal that Wells Fargo analysts say makes “strategic sense” for both sides.

items.title: Make the Most of Your Retirement with These Top-Ranked Mutual Funds
items.description: Adding these three top-ranked, best-performing, and well-managed mutual funds to your retirement portfolio could maximize your returns.

items.title: 5 Must-Buy Investment Bank Behemoths on a Positive Industry Scenario
items.description: Investment banks surge in 2025 as M&A rebounds, AI boosts efficiency, and GS, JPM, C, EVR and IBKR shine.
